findings.title: Reporter-priced assets are live at a hardcoded price of 1 until their first reporter update

best_agent_match.title: Reporter-backed assets start at a hardcoded price of 1, enabling catastrophic mispricing before first feed updates

findings.title: Failover mode turns the Uniswap TWAP from a guardrail into the authoritative price source

best_agent_match.title: Failover mode lets anyone push the official price directly to the Uniswap TWAP

findings.title: Extreme but valid TWAP values can overflow intermediate arithmetic and brick oracle updates

best_agent_match.title: Unsafe intermediate multiplications allow extreme but valid TWAPs to brick price updates

findings.title: Stale reporter prices can remain authoritative indefinitely because the oracle tracks neither freshness nor round progression

best_agent_match.title: No freshness tracking allows stale or replayed reporter prices to remain authoritative indefinitely

findings.title: Constructor never authenticates that a configured anchor address is the intended Uniswap pool for the asset

best_agent_match.title: The anchor pool address is never validated as the intended Uniswap pair or even as a genuine pool

findings.title: Duplicate config keys are silently shadowed because all lookups return the first match

best_agent_match.title: Config keys are not required to be unique, so first-match lookups can silently shadow live markets

rejected_candidates.title: Uninitialized return value in validate function
rejected_candidates.reason: Incorrect. In Solidity 0.8, the named return variable `valid` is default-initialized to `false`; the guarded branch does not return random or uninitialized data.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing access control on pokeFailedOverPrice
rejected_candidates.reason: Permissionless access is intentional and only becomes dangerous because failover switches the oracle to the TWAP source. That risk is already captured in F-002; the timing-only aspect is not a separate reportable issue.

rejected_candidates.title: No reverts on invalid or non-existent Uniswap pools
rejected_candidates.reason: The claim is misstated: bad pool addresses will generally revert once `observe()` is called. The real issue is missing constructor validation of the intended pool identity, which is captured more accurately in F-005.

rejected_candidates.title: Potential precision loss in TWAP calculation
rejected_candidates.reason: Minor integer rounding from tick averaging is expected in fixed-point oracle math and does not create a realistic exploit or protocol-level harm by itself.

rejected_candidates.title: Off-by-one in anchor ratio bounds
rejected_candidates.reason: Incorrect. When tolerance is zero, both bounds equal `1e18`, and an exact reporter/anchor match passes because the check is `<= upper` and `>= lower`.
